name: extract-companies-batch
description: Identify the company that raised funding from a batch of (title, snippet) pairs returned by a Google news search. Returns {company, is_funding} per item. Handles roundups, investor-led syntax, generic/publisher titles, possessive prefixes, title-snippet conflicts, and aggregator listings.

iteration_log.mutation: explicit decision rules; title-vs-snippet priority; investor-led/bullet/possessive guidance
v002 + 11 few-shot examples covering each failure class
clarify is_funding independence from company=null; add 'eyes' verb example; add 4 roundup-still-funding examples
SUBTRACTIVE — removed 4 examples; rejected (dropped below threshold)

input_schema.items: list of {title: string, snippet: string} (max 25 per batch)

output_schema.results: list of {idx: int (1-indexed in batch), company: string|null, is_funding: bool}

scoring.company_match_rule: case-insensitive, whitespace-normalized exact match. null and empty string are equivalent. Returning any string when null was expected is a fail.
